This app has potential, but it’s super confusing and has serious latency for everyone but the performer. Friends and I tried to hold a private party and it took us so long just to figure out how to play a song and find the mic that people had already left out of frustration. And when we did figure it out, we all heard the singer’s voice a full 1-2 seconds behind the track. So right now it’s just not feasible unless the track is presented in sync with the performer’s voice. It’s a shame, because the environment is cool - but I’d definitely suggest some popup navigation guides. I don’t know if it’s even possible to fix the latency, but it’s a real issue that I can’t believe no one else has mentioned…

One of the most exciting parts of Stage Time is the variety of people you meet. You can run into someone from across the globe, strike up a conversation, share a laugh, or even build long‑term friendships. It’s a place where personalities shine, creativity thrives, and every session feels different because of the people you encounter. What makes it even more special is the potential it has. The community is growing, the events keep getting better. It’s easy to feel excited about the future—more features, more events, and more chances to connect with new friends. For many players, Stage Time isn’t just a game it’s a space to belong, to express yourself, and to build memories with people you might never have met otherwise.

Stage Time VR is a game that makes me feel two completely different emotions at the same time. I love it because it is fun, creative, and exciting, but I also hate it because it has caused me frustration — especially after I got banned for a whole year. That ban made me angry and sad, yet it also showed how much the game meant to me.

One reason I hate Stage Time VR is because things can go wrong easily. Players sometimes break rules, start drama, or ruin performances, and when that happens, it stops being fun. Getting banned for a year was the worst part. It felt unfair, and losing access to a game I enjoyed so much made me feel left out. Watching others play while I couldn’t join was really frustrating.

At the same time, I still love Stage Time VR. Before the ban, I had great moments performing, meeting people, and improving my confidence. The game helped me be creative and brave enough to stand on a virtual stage. It wasn’t just a game to me — it was a place where I could express myself and have fun.

Even though I’m upset about being banned, I can’t pretend I don’t care about Stage Time VR. If I truly hated it, the ban wouldn’t hurt so much. The truth is, I hate the bad experiences, but I love the good memories and the joy the game brought me. That’s why Stage Time VR will always be a game I both dislike and love at the same time.

Hello, this is my updated review as of right now on January 13th, 2026. I have mixed opinions on this vr game, this game is not so mid-good and imo it’s only good because of all the people that you can meet, you can meet awesome people but on the other side you can meet genuinely horrible people that no one talks about. The owner of the game could be a nice dude, but he really doesn’t care about the community, tox mod used to be super duper sensitive and would literally ban everyone but as of right now, tox mod barely works anymore and that kinda sucks, you can literally find someone spam n words and you will see them the week later. But at least they added a built in report system and you can report them manually which can be useful but really annoying. The other maps feel like they are just filler maps, the only popular ones are st square and nyans
